title: Flight Recorder integration: span lifecycle emits FR-EVT-SPAN-* via registry
goal: Wire MT-196 spans to MT-198 registry by automatically emitting FR-EVT-SPAN-{STARTED,ENDED,FAILED} on every span lifecycle transition. Provides the universal observability fabric: every ActivitySpan and ModelSessionSpan boundary becomes an FR event row, queryable alongside session state for full multi-session swarm visibility.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/fr_emitter.rs with `SpanFrEmitter`, `FrRecorder` trait; updates to src/backend/handshake_core/src/observability/spans.rs to call emitter on lifecycle transitions; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/observability_fr_integration_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-196 spans + MT-197 repo + MT-198 registry all merged.
implementation_notes: Define `FrRecorder` trait: `fn record(event_id: FrEventId, payload: serde_json::Value, span_context: Option<SpanContextRef>) -> Result<(), FrRecorderError>`. Default impl `PostgresFrRecorder` writes to kernel_event_ledger (KERNEL-001 existing table) with model_session_id + span_id columns populated from SpanContext. `SpanFrEmitter::on_span_start(span) -> Result<()>` invokes the recorder with FrEventId::SpanStarted + payload `{ span_id, model_session_id, parent_span_id, kind, attributes }`. on_span_end similar with FrEventId::SpanEnded + duration_ms. on_span_failed with FrEventId::SpanFailed + reason. SpanGuard Drop calls the appropriate end/failed emitter based on guard.status at drop time. Decoupled: emitter is injected into spans via a `tokio::task_local!` ref so tests can substitute a `NullFrRecorder` for unit tests and `StubFrRecorder` (in-memory vec) for assertions. Sampling: span-level sampling is supported via `SpanFrEmitterConfig { sample_rate: f32 (0.0 .. 1.0), force_emit_failures: bool (default true) }` — sampled-out spans skip emission but failures always emit (per spec §5 observability invariant: failures are never silently dropped). Integration test asserts: (a) every span boundary produces an FR event row; (b) parent_span_id correlation correct; (c) Drop-on-panic still emits SpanFailed; (d) sampling reduces span emissions but never drops failures; (e) PostgresFrRecorder write performance > 500 events/sec (uses batched insert via MT-191's channel pattern adapted).
risk_if_missed: Without automatic FR emission, spans live in their own silo unjoinable with the EventLedger; the cluster X observability slice fails to deliver the 'session-wide queries' invariant; multi-session debugging requires manual correlation queries.
validator_focus: SpanGuard Drop ALWAYS emits an event (even on panic — tested via catch_unwind); failures bypass sampling (force_emit_failures honored); PostgresFrRecorder uses batched async writes per spec §5.7.3 pattern (does not block span Drop).

minimum_controls: Drop-on-panic emission test asserts SpanFailed event row exists in StubFrRecorder after catch_unwind.
Sampling test asserts force_emit_failures honored even with sample_rate=0.0 — failures always emitted.
Performance test asserts > 500 events/sec sustainable via batched async write path (no per-event blocking).
